source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A bush has 10 branches. Each branch has either 5 leaves only or 2 leaves and 1 flower. Which of the following could be the total number of leaves the bush has? <image1>
extraction_notes: answer field is option letter mapped to option text

image_dependency: question_text is fully understandable without referring to any diagram or visual attributes
requires_image=false and text_dominant=true indicate the problem is primarily text-based
although image_manifest contains 1 image, no image-dependent wording appears in the question
multi_step: solving requires considering two branch types and combining them across 10 branches
a typical solution involves forming possible leaf totals from repeated branch choices, then checking them against the answer options
the task is not single-fact recall; it needs structured enumeration or a simple expression plus comparison to choices
verifiability: this is a multiple-choice-style problem with explicit choices and a clear final answer
the answer can be checked by deterministic arithmetic on the branch counts
answer_present=true and registry_passed=true support reliable downstream checking

decision_reasons: question, answer, and image assets are all present with no registry issues
verifiability is high (0.95), so the final answer can be checked reliably
the problem appears fully understandable from text alone, reducing dependence on the image asset
multi-step reasoning demand is meaningful (0.64), supporting candidate value
noted risk flags are minor metadata/format concerns rather than missing core semantics
